concepts.title: Multiplication of two determinant
concepts.conceptBody:
Evaluation of determinant

While evaluating the determinants we use some following notations:

For example:

(1) ${{ ext{R}}_{ ext{i}}}$ denotes the ${{ ext{i}}^{ ext{th }}}$ row $ ext{ }\!\!\And\!\! ext{ }{{ ext{C}}_{ ext{i}}}$ denotes the ${{ ext{i}}^{ ext{th }}}$ column

(2) ${{R}_{i}}\leftrightarrow {{R}_{j}}$ denotes the exchange of ${{i}^{ ext{th }}}$ and ${{j}^{ ext{th }}}$ row and ${{C}_{i}}\leftrightarrow {{C}_{j}}$ denotes the exchange of ${{i}^{ ext{th }}}$ $ ext{ }\!\!\And\!\! ext{ }{{ ext{j}}^{ ext{th }}}$ column.

(3) ${{ ext{R}}_{ ext{i}}} o {{ ext{R}}_{ ext{i}}}+\lambda {{ ext{R}}_{ ext{j}}}$ denotes the addition of $\lambda $ times the element of ${{ ext{j}}^{ ext{th }}}$ row.

Similarly ${{\mathbf{C}}_{ ext{i}}} o {{\mathbf{C}}_{ ext{i}}}+\lambda {{\mathbf{C}}_{ ext{j}}}$

(4) ${{ ext{R}}_{ ext{i}}} o \lambda {{ ext{R}}_{ ext{i}}}$ denotes the multiplication of all elements of ${{ ext{i}}^{ ext{th }}}$ row by $\lambda $.

Trick: In evaluating determinants we always try to bring as many zeros as possible by using the properties we did earlier. 

Product of two determinants

Let $\quad {{\Delta }_{1}}=\left| egin{array}{*{35}{l}} {{ ext{a}}_{1}} & {{ ext{a}}_{2}} & {{ ext{a}}_{8}} \ ~{{ ext{b}}_{1}} & ~{{ ext{b}}_{2}} & ~{{ ext{b}}_{3}} \ {{ ext{c}}_{1}} & {{ ext{c}}_{2}} & {{ ext{c}}_{3}} \ \end{array} ight|$ and ${{\Delta }_{2}}=\left| egin{array}{*{35}{l}} {{ ext{x}}_{1}} & {{ ext{x}}_{2}} & {{ ext{x}}_{3}} \ {{ ext{y}}_{1}} & {{ ext{y}}_{2}} & {{ ext{y}}_{3}} \ {{ ext{z}}_{1}} & {{ ext{z}}_{2}} & {{ ext{z}}_{3}} \ \end{array} ight|$

then product of ${{\Delta }_{1}}$ and ${{\Delta }_{2}}$ is defined as

${{\Delta }_{1}}{{\Delta }_{2}}=\left| egin{array}{*{35}{l}} {{a}_{1}}{{x}_{1}}+{{a}_{2}}{{x}_{2}}+{{a}_{3}}{{x}_{3}} & {{a}_{1}}{{y}_{1}}+{{a}_{2}}{{y}_{2}}+{{a}_{3}}{{y}_{3}} & {{a}_{1}}{{z}_{1}}+{{a}_{2}}{{z}_{2}}+{{a}_{3}}{{z}_{3}} \ {{b}_{1}}{{x}_{1}}+{{b}_{2}}{{x}_{2}}+{{b}_{3}}{{x}_{3}} & {{b}_{1}}{{y}_{1}}+{{b}_{2}}{{y}_{2}}+{{b}_{3}}{{y}_{3}} & {{b}_{1}}{{z}_{1}}+{{b}_{2}}{{z}_{2}}+{{b}_{3}}{{z}_{3}} \ {{c}_{1}}{{x}_{1}}+{{c}_{2}}{{x}_{2}}+{{c}_{3}}{{x}_{3}} & {{c}_{1}}{{y}_{1}}+{{c}_{2}}{{y}_{2}}+{{c}_{3}}{{y}_{3}} & {{c}_{1}}{{z}_{1}}+{{c}_{2}}{{z}_{2}}+{{c}_{3}}{{z}_{3}} \ \end{array} ight|$

Example: Let ${{\Delta }_{1}}=\left| egin{matrix} 1 & 2 & 3 \ -2 & 3 & 5 \ 0 & 2 & 1 \ \end{matrix} ight|={{\Delta }_{2}}=\left| egin{matrix} 3 & 4 & -1 \ 0 & -1 & 2 \ -3 & 2 & 6 \ \end{matrix} ight|$

$ ext{ Then }\quad {{\Delta }_{1}}{{\Delta }_{2}}=\left| egin{matrix} 1.3+2.4+3(-1) & 1.0+2(-1)+3.2 & 1(-3)+2.2+3.6 \ (-2).3+3.4+5(-1) & (-2).0+3(-1)+5.2 & (-2)(-3)+3.2+5.6 \ 0.3+2.4+1(-1) & 0.0+2(-1)+1.2 & 0(-3)+2.2+1.6 \ \end{matrix} ight|$

$=\left| egin{array}{*{35}{l}} 8 & 4 & 19 \ 1 & 7 & 42 \ 7 & 0 & 10 \ \end{array} ight|$

Note: (i) Here we have multiplied rows by rows. Since value of a determinant does not change when rows and columns are interchanged, therefore, while finding the product of two determinants, we can also multiply rows by columns or columns by columns.

Note: (ii) \[{{\Delta }_{1}}{{\Delta }_{2}}={{\Delta }_{2}}{{\Delta }_{1}}\] (since in a determinant rows and columns can be interchanged)

An important result

If$\Delta =\left| egin{array}{*{35}{l}} {{ ext{a}}_{1}} & {{ ext{a}}_{2}} & {{ ext{a}}_{3}} \ ~{{ ext{b}}_{1}} & ~{{ ext{b}}_{2}} & ~{{ ext{b}}_{3}} \ {{ ext{c}}_{1}} & {{ ext{c}}_{2}} & {{ ext{c}}_{3}} \ \end{array} ight|$ then $\left| egin{array}{*{35}{l}} {{ ext{A}}_{1}} & ~{{ ext{A}}_{2}} & ~{{ ext{A}}_{3}} \ ~{{ ext{B}}_{1}} & ~{{ ext{B}}_{2}} & ~{{ ext{B}}_{3}} \ {{ ext{C}}_{1}} & {{ ext{C}}_{2}} & {{ ext{C}}_{3}} \ \end{array} ight|={{\Delta }^{2}}$

Where capital letters denote the cofactors of corresponding small letters in $\Delta $ i.e. ${{ ext{A}}_{ ext{i}}}=$ cofactor of ${{a}_{i}},{{B}_{i}}=$ cofactor of ${{b}_{i}}$ and ${{c}_{i}}=$ cofactor of ${{c}_{i}}$ in the determinant $\Delta $. We have seen that

${{ ext{a}}_{1}}~{{ ext{A}}_{1}}+{{ ext{a}}_{2}}~{{ ext{A}}_{2}}+{{ ext{a}}_{3}}~{{ ext{A}}_{3}}=\Delta $

${{ ext{b}}_{1}}~{{ ext{B}}_{1}}+{{ ext{b}}_{2}}~{{ ext{B}}_{2}}+{{ ext{b}}_{3}}~{{ ext{B}}_{3}}=\Delta $

${{ ext{c}}_{1}}{{ ext{C}}_{1}}+{{ ext{c}}_{2}}{{ ext{C}}_{2}}+{{ ext{c}}_{3}}{{ ext{C}}_{3}}=\Delta $

and ${{ ext{a}}_{1}}~{{ ext{B}}_{1}}+{{ ext{a}}_{2}}~{{ ext{B}}_{2}}+{{ ext{a}}_{3}}~{{ ext{B}}_{3}}=0$

${{ ext{b}}_{1}}~{{ ext{A}}_{1}}+{{ ext{b}}_{2}}~{{ ext{A}}_{2}}+{{ ext{b}}_{3}}~{{ ext{A}}_{3}} ext{ }=0$

${{ ext{a}}_{1}}{{ ext{C}}_{1}}+{{ ext{a}}_{2}}{{ ext{C}}_{2}}+{{ ext{a}}_{3}}{{ ext{C}}_{3}} ext{ }=0$

${{c}_{1}}{{A}_{1}}+{{c}_{2}}{{A}_{2}}+{{c}_{3}}{{A}_{3}}=0$

${{b}_{1}}{{C}_{1}}+{{b}_{2}}{{C}_{2}}+{{b}_{3}}{{C}_{3}}=0$

${{c}_{1}}{{B}_{1}}+{{c}_{2}}{{B}_{2}}+{{c}_{3}}{{B}_{3}}=0$

Case I. When $\Delta eq 0$

Let ${{\Delta }_{1}}=\left| egin{array}{*{35}{l}} {{ ext{A}}_{1}} & ~{{ ext{A}}_{2}} & ~{{ ext{A}}_{3}} \ ~{{ ext{B}}_{1}} & ~{{ ext{B}}_{2}} & ~{{ ext{B}}_{3}} \ {{ ext{C}}_{1}} & {{ ext{C}}_{2}} & {{ ext{C}}_{3}} \ \end{array} ight|$

Now $\Delta {{\Delta }_{1}}=\left| egin{array}{*{35}{l}} {{a}_{1}} & {{a}_{2}} & {{a}_{3}} \ {{b}_{1}} & {{b}_{2}} & {{b}_{3}} \ {{c}_{1}} & {{c}_{2}} & {{c}_{3}} \ \end{array} ight|\left| egin{array}{*{35}{l}} {{A}_{1}} & {{A}_{2}} & {{A}_{3}} \ {{B}_{1}} & {{B}_{2}} & {{B}_{3}} \ {{C}_{1}} & {{C}_{2}} & {{C}_{3}} \ \end{array} ight|$

$\left| egin{array}{*{35}{l}} {{a}_{1}}{{A}_{1}}+{{a}_{2}}{{A}_{2}}+{{a}_{3}}{{A}_{3}} & {{a}_{1}}{{B}_{1}}+{{a}_{2}}{{B}_{2}}+{{a}_{3}}{{B}_{3}} & {{a}_{1}}{{C}_{1}}+{{a}_{2}}{{C}_{2}}+{{a}_{3}}{{C}_{3}} \ {{b}_{1}}{{A}_{1}}+{{b}_{2}}{{A}_{2}}+{{b}_{3}}{{A}_{3}} & {{b}_{1}}{{B}_{1}}+{{b}_{2}}{{B}_{2}}+{{b}_{3}}{{B}_{3}} & {{b}_{1}}{{C}_{1}}+{{b}_{2}}{{B}_{2}}+{{b}_{3}}{{C}_{3}} \ {{c}_{1}}{{A}_{1}}+{{c}_{2}}{{A}_{2}}+{{c}_{1}}{{A}_{3}} & {{c}_{1}}{{B}_{1}}+{{c}_{2}}{{B}_{2}}+{{a}_{3}}{{B}_{3}} & {{c}_{1}}{{C}_{1}}+{{c}_{2}}{{C}_{2}}+{{c}_{3}}{{C}_{3}} \ \end{array} ight|$

$=\left| egin{array}{*{35}{l}} \Delta & 0 & 0 \ 0 & \Delta & 0 \ 0 & 0 & \Delta \ \end{array} ight|={{\Delta }^{3}}$

Thus $\Delta {{\Delta }_{1}}={{\Delta }^{3}}\quad \Rightarrow \quad {{\Delta }_{1}}={{\Delta }^{2}}\quad [ ecause \Delta e 0]$

Case II. When $\Delta =0$

Sub. Case (i) When all elements of the determinant $\Delta $ are zero. In this case cofactors of each element of the determinant $\Delta $ will be zero and hence ${{\Delta }_{1}}=0$

$\left. herefore \quad {{\Delta }_{1}}={{\Delta }^{2}}\quad [ ext{Here}{{\Delta }_{ ext{1}}} ext{=}{{\Delta }^{ ext{2}}} ext{=0} ight]$

Sub. Case (ii) When at least one element of the determinant$\Delta $is non-zero. There is no loss of generality in assuming that ${{a}_{1}} e 0$.

Now $\left. egin{array}{*{35}{r}} {{ ext{a}}_{1}}~{{ ext{A}}_{1}}+{{ ext{a}}_{2}}~{{ ext{A}}_{2}}+{{ ext{a}}_{3}}~{{ ext{A}}_{3}}=\Delta =0 \ {{ ext{a}}_{1}}~{{ ext{B}}_{1}}+{{ ext{a}}_{2}}~{{ ext{B}}_{2}}+{{ ext{a}}_{3}}~{{ ext{B}}_{3}}=0 \ {{ ext{a}}_{1}}{{ ext{C}}_{1}}+{{ ext{a}}_{2}}{{ ext{C}}_{2}}+{{ ext{a}}_{3}}{{ ext{C}}_{3}}=0 \ \end{array} ight\}$ …(i)

(i) is system of homogeneous linear equations in${{a}_{1}},{{a}_{2}},{{a}_{3}}$ where ${{a}_{1}} e 0$, therefore it has nontrivial solution.

$ herefore \quad \left| egin{array}{*{35}{l}} {{ ext{A}}_{1}} & ~{{ ext{A}}_{2}} & ~{{ ext{A}}_{3}} \ ~{{ ext{B}}_{1}} & ~{{ ext{B}}_{2}} & ~{{ ext{B}}_{3}} \ {{ ext{C}}_{1}} & {{ ext{C}}_{2}} & {{ ext{C}}_{3}} \ \end{array} ight|=0\Rightarrow {{\Delta }_{1}}=0$

Thus ${{\Delta }_{1}}={{\Delta }^{2}}\quad \left[ herefore \Delta =0 ight.$ and $\left. {{\Delta }_{1}}=0 ight]$
